limitations: AI investment intensity scored 3 (not 4) because the 1,900 MW figure is a proposed/pending regulatory filing, not yet a committed or approved capital expenditure; no dollar amount for AI-attributable capex is disclosed in these excerpts.

Xcel Energy is a utility — AI is a demand driver for its grid/infrastructure investment, not a technology it builds or deploys internally; investment intensity reflects grid expansion to serve AI customers rather than direct AI R&D or AI compute spend.

Narrative concreteness scored 4 based on a named, in-progress commercial agreement with an identified customer (Google), though quantified business results (revenue, load volumes) are not yet disclosed.

Risk disclosure scored 4 because the excerpts describe specific mechanisms (supply chain exposure, IT/OT environment impact, increased attack sophistication) but no quantification of risk or named governance/oversight body is present.
